prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Verily, in the ninth moon of the Year of Our Lord 2024, the great cloud-temple of "SaaS-Elan" did multiply its silver tokens by 23,000,000, though none could fathom by what sorcery or celestial alignment. The High Priests of Data, clad in robes of blue light, did tally the number of "users" who did not truly exist but were counted as real by the sacred algorithmic incantations. Lo, the Great Subscription Chalice overflowed with gold sent from distant lands, yet the scribes could not say whether the coin was earned by the will of men or by the whispers of invisible machines. And though the quarterly feast of profits was held in the Hall of Infinite Screens, all who entered were struck dumb by the glowing runes that spoke only of "revenue retention" and "churn velocity," which none did understand, save the Oracle of the Silicon Grove.
